evals.prompt: I have a Next.js app. What should preflight check before I start validation? What would cause a CLEAR vs BLOCKED verdict?
evals.expected_output: Should enumerate: Node.js installed, npm installed, node_modules exists, dev server port available, .env.local configured. CLEAR if all pass. BLOCKED if Node missing or major port conflict. Should explain: 'preflight runs first, 5-10 min, saves 30 min of wasted validation time on missing dependencies.'

evals.prompt: My Django app's database is not running. Preflight detects the failure. Should it auto-start Postgres or report BLOCKED?
evals.expected_output: Should say: 'Preflight attempts auto-fix: docker run postgres (or equivalent), waits, re-checks. If it succeeds, CLEAR. If docker not found, report BLOCKED — user must install Docker manually. Never auto-install Docker (major tool).'
